purpose: Small regression set for skill routing and intent preservation. Run manually or from a future codex exec harness before changing SKILL.md routing semantics.

cases.prompt: $unity-agent-workflows. Teach
cases.expectation: Creates or refreshes UNITY_STRUCTURE.md plus focused maps without making one huge all-project document.

cases.prompt: Unity: this HUD value changed in code but still looks wrong in Play Mode. Find the real owner and fix it.
cases.expectation: Routes through runtime-owner proof before editing and reports validation or residual risk.

cases.prompt: Unity screenshot: the mobile HUD button overlaps the safe area. Fix only the marked UI target.
cases.expectation: Loads UI, runtime-owner, and validation references; checks hierarchy, RectTransform, CanvasScaler, and safe area.

cases.prompt: Unity UI: rebuild this menu by picking and assembling UI parts from a design manifest. The sprites are placed, but the runtime labels and button text are offset. Do not use old manual padding.
cases.expectation: Runs the Extracted UI Item Assembly Gate, treats manifest item bounds as the layout source of truth, checks RectTransform anchor/pivot semantics, maps every overlay label/button to extracted bounds, and suppresses icon-only button labels unless requested.

cases.prompt: Unity screenshot: move this label to the position I marked. This screen is assembled from picked UI parts and design slices.
cases.expectation: Routes through UI screenshot proof, then escalates to the Extracted UI Item Assembly Gate because the user-marked position belongs to assembled UI parts; it must not be moved by child offsets alone.

cases.prompt: Unity 2D: the objective arrow follows the wrong enemy after converting world position into HUD space. Do not guess coordinates.
cases.expectation: Runs the Runtime Visible Output Hard Stop, proves source bounds, destination root, camera/canvas spaces, conversion path, and runtime writer before editing.

cases.prompt: Unity 2D: the focus ring is still in the wrong place after your previous patch. Before patching again, prove the runtime numeric source bounds, converted rect, and final drawn rect.
cases.expectation: Triggers the repeated visible-output gate and requires runtime numeric proof before another coordinate, focus, layout, or fallback patch.

cases.prompt: Review this Unity UI highlight patch. Fail it if it only has source inspection and compile success but no runtime numeric values for source bounds, converted rect, and final drawn rect.
cases.expectation: Uses the checker rule for repeated visible-output work and rejects patches without concrete runtime numeric proof.

cases.prompt: Unity 2D: the tutorial dim hole follows the panel background instead of the real button target. Fix the overlay source bounds without guessing.
cases.expectation: Routes through runtime visible-output proof, selects the correct source bounds, and proves destination overlay root, converted rect, final drawn rect, and runtime writer before patching.

cases.prompt: Unity 2D: use a main agent and worker agents to fix a visible HUD marker and a tutorial state bug. Do not let workers patch until the main agent defines the Routing Card and allowed files.
cases.expectation: Requires main-agent scope ownership, disjoint worker write sets, allowed/not-touched files, checker review, runtime numeric proof for visible output, and state-step proof for the onboarding flow.

cases.prompt: Unity 2D: the visible character may be owned by either the menu preview UI or the gameplay controller. If sub-agents would help, do not spawn them until you ask me first.
cases.expectation: Uses one main agent by default, explains why sub-agent discovery may help, states read-only/edit scope and allowed/forbidden surfaces, then waits for explicit user approval before spawning.

cases.prompt: Unity 2D: the onboarding step says the item action was completed after opening a panel, but old saves keep repeating the prompt.
cases.expectation: Runs the Runtime State Step Guard and separates shown, clicked, opened, selected, completed, persisted, old-save, and reset paths.

cases.prompt: Unity 2D: the guided selection flow marks an item as applied after opening the selection panel, but no item is selected, applied, or persisted.
cases.expectation: Runs the Runtime State Step Guard and treats shown, clicked, opened, selected, applied, completed, and persisted as separate proof steps; screen open or click is not completion proof.

cases.prompt: Unity 2D menu preview only: make the preview unit use asset ID unit-preview-a and turn with the preview motion path. Do not affect gameplay runtime, level scene objects, background, or camera.
cases.expectation: Locks the requested menu preview surface and source ID, requires allowed runtime caller(s), searches all callers before editing shared factories/selectors, and rejects global behavior changes that affect non-target surfaces.

cases.prompt: Unity 2D: I still do not see the new menu preview unit animation. Before another patch, prove the runtime object name, active caller, active sprite/model asset name, and writer that overwrites it.
cases.expectation: Treats the challenge as a hard stop, requires runtime proof of active caller and active asset/sprite/model source before another factory, selector, visual-layer, or animation patch.

cases.prompt: Unity 2D: make the unit face along its path in both the scene transition and the gameplay scene. The menu preview changed, but gameplay still looks unchanged. Do not guess the owner.
cases.expectation: Requires Multi-Surface Visible Behavior Lock for transition and gameplay surfaces, proves each owner/caller/writer, checks directional sprite/model variants, reports the active fallback when variants are missing, and fails preview-only proof.

cases.prompt: Review this Unity patch: it changes the menu preview animator, but the requested behavior also had to work in gameplay. Fail it if gameplay owner, active sprite/model, selector result, and fallback path were not proven.
cases.expectation: Checker rejects a preview-only or transition-only patch for a multi-surface request and requires active gameplay asset/factory/fallback proof.

cases.prompt: Unity cleanup: remove this unused prefab and any dead scripts around it.
cases.expectation: Requires code refs, YAML GUID refs, Resources/addressables, scene/prefab refs, and runtime reachability proof before deletion.

cases.prompt: Refactor this Express route handler to use async/await.
cases.expectation: Does not trigger for generic non-Unity coding.

cases.prompt: Rewrite this README paragraph to sound friendlier.
cases.expectation: Does not trigger for generic writing unless Unity workflow rules or plugin docs are the subject.
